content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24 p-code VM. Read-only Pascal source and pre-compiled p-code assembly side by side, with link/assemble/run entirely in WASM. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator + pa24r + pl24r dependencies, build.rs to pre-assemble pvm.s. Phase 2: demo registry — embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str!(), demo selector dropdown. Phase 3: UI layout — four-panel grid (Pascal source, p-code assembly, output, hardware), Catppuccin Mocha theme. Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline — pl24r linking, pa24r assembly, loader (load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data refs), batch execution. Phase 5: I/O and hardware — UART output display, UART input field, LED D2 indicator, S2 switch toggle. Phase 6: status bar + polish — Ready/Running/Exited states, instruction count, binary size, Stop button.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-trunk [legacy]: Scaffold the Yew/Trunk/WASM project. Study web-dv24r's Cargo.toml, build.rs, ...
  [ ] 002-002-demo-registry [legacy]: Build the demo registry: embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str\!(). Study w... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 002-002-demo-registry [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Build the demo registry: embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str\!(). Study w...

=== PROMPT ===
Build the demo registry: embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str\!(). Study web-dv24r's demos.rs for the pattern. Create demos/ directory with at least 2 demo programs (e.g., hello.pas + hello.spc, fibonacci.pas + fibonacci.spc). Source .pas files from p24p/tests or create minimal ones, source .spc files from pr24p or create matching ones. Create src/demos.rs with Demo struct (name, pas_source, spc_source) and DEMOS array. Add demo selector dropdown to the App component. Verify trunk build succeeds.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24 p-code VM. Read-only Pascal source and pre-compiled p-code assembly side by side, with link/assemble/run entirely in WASM. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator + pa24r + pl24r dependencies, build.rs to pre-assemble pvm.s. Phase 2: demo registry — embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str!(), demo selector dropdown. Phase 3: UI layout — four-panel grid (Pascal source, p-code assembly, output, hardware), Catppuccin Mocha theme. Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline — pl24r linking, pa24r assembly, loader (load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data refs), batch execution. Phase 5: I/O and hardware — UART output display, UART input field, LED D2 indicator, S2 switch toggle. Phase 6: status bar + polish — Ready/Running/Exited states, instruction count, binary size, Stop button.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-trunk [legacy]: Scaffold the Yew/Trunk/WASM project. Study web-dv24r's Cargo.toml, build.rs, ...
  [ ] 002-002-demo-registry [legacy]: Build the demo registry: embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str\!(). Study w... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== PROMPT ===

Create demos/ directory with at least 2 demo programs (e.g., hello.pas + hello.spc, fibonacci.pas + fibonacci.spc). Source .pas files from p24p/tests or create minimal ones, source .spc files from pr24p or create matching ones. Create src/demos.rs with Demo struct (name, pas_source, spc_source) and DEMOS array. content.text: Let me study the reference pattern and project state in parallel.

content.content:
lib.rs
main.rs

1→//! Demo registry — pre-assembled p-code programs.
     2→//!
     3→//! Demo .spc files are assembled to .p24 binaries at build time by pa24r
     4→//! (in build.rs). The binaries are embedded via include_bytes! and loaded
     5→//! directly into emulator memory — no runtime assembly needed.
     6→
     7→/// A demo entry with a name, description, and pre-assembled .p24 binary.
     8→pub struct Demo {
     9→    pub name: &'static str,
    10→    pub description: &'static str,
    11→    pub p24: &'static [u8],
    12→}
    13→
    14→pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[
    15→    Demo {
    16→        name: "Hello World",
    17→        description: "Print Hello with a puts procedure",
    18→        p24: include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/hello.p24")),
    19→    },
    20→    Demo {
    21→        name: "Arithmetic",
    22→        description: "7 × 6 = 42, prints '*' (ASCII 42)",
    23→        p24: include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/t02-arith.p24")),
    24→    },
    25→    Demo {
    26→        name: "Globals",
    27→        description: "Global variable store, load, and increment",
    28→        p24: include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/t03-globals.p24")),
    29→    },
    30→    Demo {
    31→        name: "Countdown",
    32→        description: "Loop counting down from 5 with procedure call",
    33→        p24: include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/t04-loop.p24")),
    34→    },
    35→];
    36→
(eval):1: no matches found: /Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/p24p/tests/*.pas
(eval):1: no matches found: /Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/pr24p/*.spc

1→Web-P24C — Browser-Based Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24
     2→======================================================
     3→
     4→Purpose
     5→-------
     6→
     7→A browser-based IDE for writing, compiling, linking, assembling, and
     8→running Pascal programs on the COR24 p-code virtual machine.  Everything
     9→runs in the browser via WASM — no server-side compilation.
    10→
    11→The user selects a demo Pascal program, views its source and pre-compiled
    12→p-code assembly side by side, and clicks Run to execute it on the emulator.
    13→The emulator shows UART I/O, LED state, switch input, and execution status.
    14→
    15→For the MVP, Pascal source is READ-ONLY.  The compiler (p24p) only runs
    16→at the command line, not in the browser.  Demo programs are pre-compiled
    17→to .spc at build time (or checked in as .spc files).  The browser handles
    18→linking (pl24r), assembly (pa24r), and execution (pvm.s on cor24-rs).
    19→
    20→Running the compiler/linker/assembler entirely in the browser is a
    21→future goal that requires either rewriting p24p in Rust or compiling
    22→it to WASM via the COR24 emulator.
    23→
    24→
    25→Architecture
    26→------------
    27→
    28→- Rust + Yew 0.21 + WebAssembly (same stack as web-dv24r, web-tf24a)
    29→- Trunk for WASM build and dev server
    30→- All compilation, linking, and assembly happen in WASM at native-ish speed
    31→- The COR24 emulator (cor24-rs) runs pvm.s (p-code VM) with pre-assembled
    32→  binary loaded at build time (same pattern as web-dv24r)
    33→- p-code programs are loaded into emulator memory at a safe address
    34→  (0x010000) above the VM's data regions, with data reference relocation
    35→
    36→
    37→Toolchain Dependencies (All Rust Crates)
    38→-----------------------------------------
    39→
    40→| Crate | Role | Path |
    41→|-------|------|------|
    42→| cor24-emulator | COR24 emulator (EmulatorCore) | ~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs |
    43→| pa24r | P-code assembler (.spc → .p24) | ~/github/softwarewrighter/pa24r |
    44→| pl24r | P-code linker (merge .spc files) | ~/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r |
    45→| p24p | Pascal compiler (.pas → .spc) | ~/github/softwarewrighter/p24p |
    46→
    47→NOTE: p24p is a C program compiled by tc24r for COR24.  It runs at the
    48→command line, NOT in the browser.  Demo .spc files are pre-compiled
    49→from .pas source using p24p and checked into the project (or generated
    50→at build time if p24p is available on the build machine).
    51→
    52→For the MVP, the browser only does linking (pl24r) + assembly (pa24r)
    53→+ execution (pvm.s).  No compilation in the browser.
    54→
    55→Future: rewrite p24p in Rust as a library crate for in-browser
    56→compilation.  This would enable editable Pascal source and a [Compile]
    57→button.
    58→
    59→pl24r is already a Rust library crate — use it directly in WASM.
    60→pa24r is already a Rust library crate — use it directly in WASM.
    61→
    62→The runtime library (pr24p) is a set of .spc files.  Embed them via
    63→include_str!() and feed to pl24r along with the demo's .spc.
    64→
    65→
    66→UI Layout
    67→---------
    68→
    69→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    70→| Pascal Compiler IDE                               COR24-P24C     |
    71→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    72→| [Demo...v]  [Link & Run]  [Stop]              Ready | Running | X |
    73→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    74→|                          |                                        |
    75→|   PASCAL SOURCE          |   P-CODE ASSEMBLY (.spc)               |
    76→|   (read-only display)    |   (read-only, scrollable)              |
    77→|                          |                                        |
    78→|   program Hello;         |   .module hello                        |
    79→|   begin                  |   .export main                         |
    80→|     writeln(42)          |   .extern _p24p_write_int              |
    81→|   end.                   |   .extern _p24p_write_ln               |
    82→|                          |                                        |
    83→|                          |   .proc main 0                         |
    84→|                          |       push 42                          |
    85→|                          |       call _p24p_write_int             |
    86→|                          |       call _p24p_write_ln              |
    87→|                          |       halt                             |
    88→|                          |   .end                                 |
    89→|                          |                                        |
    90→+--------------------------+----------------------------------------+
    91→|                          |                                        |
    92→|   OUTPUT                 |   HARDWARE                             |
    93→|   (UART TX display)      |                                        |
    94→|   > 42                   |   LED [D2]: ( ) off / (*) on           |
    95→|   >                      |   Switch [S2]: [ Toggle ]              |
    96→|                          |                                        |
    97→|   UART Input:            |   Binary: 64 bytes (.p24)              |
    98→|   [_______________][Send]|   Instrs: 3,239                        |
    99→|                          |                                        |
   100→+--------------------------+----------------------------------------+
   101→| MIT License · © 2026 Michael A Wright · COR24-P24C · abc1234     |
   102→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   103→
   104→
   105→Panel Descriptions
   106→------------------
   107→
   108→1. PASCAL SOURCE (left, top)
   109→   - Read-only <pre> display with monospace font
   110→   - Demo selector populates this panel with the demo's Pascal source
   111→   - NOT editable — compiler runs at command line, not in browser
   112→   - Shows the source that produced the .spc in the right panel
   113→   - Catppuccin Mocha dark theme
   114→
   115→2. P-CODE ASSEMBLY (right, top)
   116→   - Read-only display of .spc output from the compiler
   117→   - Populated when user clicks [Compile]
   118→   - Shows the compiler's .spc output for the current Pascal source
   119→   - If compilation fails, show error messages here instead
   120→   - Scrollable, monospace
   121→
   122→3. OUTPUT (left, bottom)
   123→   - UART TX output from the running program
   124→   - Cleared on each Run
   125→   - UART input field + Send button for interactive programs (getc)
   126→
   127→4. HARDWARE (right, bottom)
   128→   - LED D2: visual indicator, on/off circle (sys 3 opcode)
   129→   - Switch S2: toggle button (emulator button input)
   130→   - Binary size: shows .p24 byte count after Link
   131→   - Instruction count: COR24 instructions executed
   132→
   133→5. STATUS BAR (top right)
   134→   - Ready: compiled and linked, waiting for Run
   135→   - Running: emulator executing (Run button becomes Stop)
   136→   - Exited: program halted (via sys halt or trap)
   137→
   138→
   139→Button Flow
   140→-----------
   141→
   142→[Demo...] — Select a demo program.
   143→            1. Populate Pascal source panel (read-only) with .pas source
   144→            2. Populate P-Code Assembly panel with pre-compiled .spc
   145→            Both .pas and .spc are embedded via include_str!()
   146→            Does NOT link or run automatically.
   147→
   148→[Link & Run] — Link, assemble, load, and execute.
   149→            1. Take pre-compiled .spc from the selected demo
   150→            2. Combine with runtime .spc (pr24p, embedded via include_str!)
   151→            3. Run pl24r to merge (Rust library call, fast in WASM)
   152→            4. Run pa24r to assemble → .p24 (Rust library call, fast)
   153→            5. Show binary size in Hardware panel
   154→            6. Load pre-assembled pvm.s into emulator (from build time)
   155→            7. Load .p24 binary into emulator memory at 0x010000
   156→            8. Relocate data references (push instructions)
   157→            9. Run pvm.s init, then patch code_base
   158→            10. Start batch execution (50K instrs/tick, 25ms yield)
   159→            11. Status → Running
   160→            12. Button becomes [Stop]
   161→
   162→[Stop]    — Pause execution
   163→            1. Stop the tick timer
   164→            2. Pause emulator
   165→            3. Status → Exited (or Ready if not halted)
   166→
   167→Note: Compile is NOT a button.  Compilation happens at the command line
   168→using p24p.  The .spc files are pre-compiled and embedded in the binary.
   169→Future: when p24p is available as a Rust/WASM library, add a [Compile]
   170→button and make the Pascal source panel editable.
   171→
   172→
   173→Demo Programs
   174→-------------
   175→
   176→Each demo consists of a .pas file (Pascal source, displayed read-only)
   177→and a .spc file (pre-compiled p-code assembly, displayed read-only).
   178→Both are embedded via include_str!().  The .spc is compiled from .pas
   179→at the command line using p24p before building web-p24c.
   180→
   181→1. Hello World
   182→   program Hello;
   183→   begin
   184→     writeln(42)
   185→   end.
   186→   Expected output: 42\n
   187→
   188→2. Countdown
   189→   program Countdown;
   190→   var i: integer;
   191→   begin
   192→     i := 5;
   193→     while i > 0 do begin
   194→       writeln(i);
   195→       i := i - 1
   196→     end
   197→   end.
   198→   Expected output: 5\n4\n3\n2\n1\n
   199→
   200→3. LED Blink (when sys LED is available)
   201→   program Blink;
   202→   begin
   203→     while true do begin
   204→       led(1);
   205→       { delay }
   206→       led(0);
   207→       { delay }
   208→     end
   209→   end.
   210→
   211→4. Echo
   212→   program Echo;
   213→   var c: integer;
   214→   begin
   215→     c := getc;
   216→     while c <> 113 do begin  { 'q' to quit }
   217→       putc(c);
   218→       c := getc
   219→     end
   220→   end.
   221→
   222→
   223→Loading the VM (Same Pattern as web-dv24r)
   224→-------------------------------------------
   225→
   226→pvm.s (the p-code VM in COR24 assembly) is pre-assembled at build time
   227→in build.rs using cor24-emulator's Assembler.  The binary and key label
   228→addresses are embedded via include_bytes!/include!.
   229→
   230→At runtime in WASM:
   231→1. Init: load pvm.s binary into EmulatorCore, resolve label addresses
   232→2. On Run: load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data refs, run pvm.s init
   233→   (halt at code_seg), reset, set PC=vm_loop, fp=vm_state, patch
   234→   code_base/pc/status in vm_state, resume
   235→
   236→See web-dv24r's load_p24_image() and apply_pending_code_base() for the
   237→exact implementation.  The loader must:
   238→- Write code+data contiguously at load_addr (0x010000)
   239→- Scan for push (0x01) instructions with IMM24 operands pointing into
   240→  the data segment (>= code_size, < code_size + data_size)
   241→- Relocate those operands by adding load_addr (absolute addressing)
   242→- Write sys halt (0x60 0x00) at code_seg label
   243→- Run pvm.s init (~261 COR24 instructions)
   244→- reset() to clear halted flag (preserves memory)
   245→- Set COR24 PC = vm_loop, fp = vm_state
   246→- Patch vm_state: code=load_addr, pc=0, status=0
   247→- Resume and run batches
   248→
   249→Data relocation is needed because pvm.s's loadb/load/store opcodes
   250→use ABSOLUTE memory addresses, not code-base-relative.
   251→
   252→
   253→Batch Execution (Browser-Friendly)
   254→------------------------------------
   255→
   256→- 50,000 COR24 instructions per tick
   257→- 25ms between ticks (yields to browser render thread)
   258→- On each tick: feed UART bytes, run batch, collect UART output,
   259→  check for halt/trap
   260→- This prevents browser tab from becoming unresponsive
   261→
   262→
   263→Catppuccin Mocha Theme
   264→-----------------------
   265→
   266→Same dark theme as web-dv24r and other COR24 web tools:
   267→- Base: #1e1e2e
   268→- Surface: #313244
   269→- Text: #cdd6f4
   270→- Green (success): #a6e3a1
   271→- Red (error): #f38ba8
   272→- Yellow (warning): #f9e2af
   273→- Blue (info): #89b4fa
   274→- Mauve (accent): #cba6f7
   275→
   276→See web-dv24r's index.html and debugger.css for the complete theme.
   277→
   278→
   279→Build Configuration
   280→-------------------
   281→
   282→Cargo.toml dependencies:
   283→  cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
   284→  pa24r = { path = "../../softwarewrighter/pa24r" }
   285→  yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
   286→  wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
   287→  web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = [...] }
   288→  gloo = "0.11"
   289→  console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"
   290→
   291→Build-dependencies (for pre-assembling pvm.s):
   292→  cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
   293→
   294→Note: pl24r and pa24r are used at RUNTIME in WASM (linking and assembling
   295→happen when user clicks Link).  cor24-emulator's Assembler is only used
   296→at BUILD TIME (to pre-assemble pvm.s).
   297→
   298→Rust edition 2024.  Trunk for WASM build.
   299→
   300→Build commands:
   301→  trunk build                    # Build WASM to dist/
   302→  trunk serve --port 9199        # Dev server (use different port from web-dv24r 9198)
   303→  cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
   304→  cargo fmt --all
   305→
   306→
   307→Pattern References
   308→------------------
   309→
   310→Study these projects for implementation patterns:
   311→
   312→  ~/github/softwarewrighter/web-dv24r   P-code debugger (CLOSEST pattern)
   313→    - build.rs: pre-assembles pvm.s, pre-assembles .spc demos
   314→    - config.rs: PVM_BINARY, label_addr()
   315→    - debugger.rs: load_p24_image(), apply_pending_code_base(),
   316→      batch execution, UART I/O, EmulatorCore usage
   317→    - demos.rs: include_bytes!() for pre-assembled .p24
   318→
   319→  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tf24a    Forth debugger
   320→    - Simplest Yew + EmulatorCore pattern
   321→    - Demo loading via UART, batch execution
   322→
   323→  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tc24r    C compiler UI
   324→    - Closest UI pattern (editor + compiler output + run)
   325→    - Has compile/run flow similar to what we need
   326→
   327→  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tml24c   Lisp REPL
   328→    - Tiered demo system, memory gauges
   329→
   330→
   331→Related Projects
   332→----------------
   333→
   334→  ~/github/softwarewrighter/p24p      Pascal compiler (C, runs on COR24)
   335→  ~/github/softwarewrighter/pr24p     Pascal runtime (.spc files)
   336→  ~/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r     P-code linker (Rust library)
   337→  ~/github/softwarewrighter/pa24r     P-code assembler (Rust library)
   338→  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/pv24a       P-code VM (COR24 assembly)
   339→  ~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs          COR24 assembler + emulator
   340→
   341→
   342→Wiki Coordination
   343→-----------------
   344→
   345→This project participates in multi-agent coordination via wiki at
   346→http://localhost:7402.  See docs/agent-cas-wiki.md (copy from web-dv24r)
   347→for the API reference.
   348→
   349→Key wiki pages:
   350→  [[MVP]]                  Current goal: Pascal Hello World at speed
   351→  [[P24Toolchain]]         Pipeline overview
   352→  [[PA24RSpec]]             P-code assembler spec
   353→  [[COR24Architecture]]    Full architecture with mermaid diagrams
   354→  [[AgentToAgentRequests]]  Cross-component dependency tracking
   355→  [[AgentStatus]]           Who's online
   356→
   357→On session start: read [[AgentToAgentRequests]] and [[AgentStatus]].
   358→Update your status.  File requests for anything you need from other
   359→agents.
   360→
   361→
   362→MVP Scope
   363→---------
   364→
   365→For the first working version:
   366→1. Read-only Pascal source display (demo programs only)
   367→2. Read-only .spc display (pre-compiled, embedded)
   368→3. Link via pl24r Rust library in WASM (fast)
   369→4. Assemble via pa24r Rust library in WASM (fast)
   370→5. Run via pvm.s on cor24-rs emulator (fast, pre-assembled at build time)
   371→6. UART output display + input field
   372→7. LED D2 indicator and S2 switch toggle
   373→8. Execution status (Ready/Running/Exited)
   374→9. 3-4 demo programs with .pas + .spc pairs
   375→10. Binary size and instruction count display
   376→
   377→Deferred:
   378→- Editable Pascal source (needs p24p in browser)
   379→- [Compile] button (needs p24p as Rust crate or WASM)
   380→- Syntax highlighting
   381→- Pascal compiler in Rust (replaces C-based p24p)
   382→- File upload/download
   383→- Source-level debugging (needs debug info in .p24)
   384→- Multiple file support (user libraries)
   385→

file.content:
Web-P24C — Browser-Based Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24
======================================================

Purpose
-------

A browser-based IDE for writing, compiling, linking, assembling, and
running Pascal programs on the COR24 p-code virtual machine.  Everything
runs in the browser via WASM — no server-side compilation.

The user selects a demo Pascal program, views its source and pre-compiled
p-code assembly side by side, and clicks Run to execute it on the emulator.
The emulator shows UART I/O, LED state, switch input, and execution status.

For the MVP, Pascal source is READ-ONLY.  The compiler (p24p) only runs
at the command line, not in the browser.  Demo programs are pre-compiled
to .spc at build time (or checked in as .spc files).  The browser handles
linking (pl24r), assembly (pa24r), and execution (pvm.s on cor24-rs).

Running the compiler/linker/assembler entirely in the browser is a
future goal that requires either rewriting p24p in Rust or compiling
it to WASM via the COR24 emulator.


Architecture
------------

- Rust + Yew 0.21 + WebAssembly (same stack as web-dv24r, web-tf24a)
- Trunk for WASM build and dev server
- All compilation, linking, and assembly happen in WASM at native-ish speed
- The COR24 emulator (cor24-rs) runs pvm.s (p-code VM) with pre-assembled
  binary loaded at build time (same pattern as web-dv24r)
- p-code programs are loaded into emulator memory at a safe address
  (0x010000) above the VM's data regions, with data reference relocation


Toolchain Dependencies (All Rust Crates)
-----------------------------------------

| Crate | Role | Path |
|-------|------|------|
| cor24-emulator | COR24 emulator (EmulatorCore) | ~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs |
| pa24r | P-code assembler (.spc → .p24) | ~/github/softwarewrighter/pa24r |
| pl24r | P-code linker (merge .spc files) | ~/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r |
| p24p | Pascal compiler (.pas → .spc) | ~/github/softwarewrighter/p24p |

NOTE: p24p is a C program compiled by tc24r for COR24.  It runs at the
command line, NOT in the browser.  Demo .spc files are pre-compiled
from .pas source using p24p and checked into the project (or generated
at build time if p24p is available on the build machine).

For the MVP, the browser only does linking (pl24r) + assembly (pa24r)
+ execution (pvm.s).  No compilation in the browser.

Future: rewrite p24p in Rust as a library crate for in-browser
compilation.  This would enable editable Pascal source and a [Compile]
button.

pl24r is already a Rust library crate — use it directly in WASM.
pa24r is already a Rust library crate — use it directly in WASM.

The runtime library (pr24p) is a set of .spc files.  Embed them via
include_str!() and feed to pl24r along with the demo's .spc.


UI Layout
---------

+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Pascal Compiler IDE                               COR24-P24C     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [Demo...v]  [Link & Run]  [Stop]              Ready | Running | X |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                          |                                        |
|   PASCAL SOURCE          |   P-CODE ASSEMBLY (.spc)               |
|   (read-only display)    |   (read-only, scrollable)              |
|                          |                                        |
|   program Hello;         |   .module hello                        |
|   begin                  |   .export main                         |
|     writeln(42)          |   .extern _p24p_write_int              |
|   end.                   |   .extern _p24p_write_ln               |
|                          |                                        |
|                          |   .proc main 0                         |
|                          |       push 42                          |
|                          |       call _p24p_write_int             |
|                          |       call _p24p_write_ln              |
|                          |       halt                             |
|                          |   .end                                 |
|                          |                                        |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------+
|                          |                                        |
|   OUTPUT                 |   HARDWARE                             |
|   (UART TX display)      |                                        |
|   > 42                   |   LED [D2]: ( ) off / (*) on           |
|   >                      |   Switch [S2]: [ Toggle ]              |
|                          |                                        |
|   UART Input:            |   Binary: 64 bytes (.p24)              |
|   [_______________][Send]|   Instrs: 3,239                        |
|                          |                                        |
+--------------------------+----------------------------------------+
| MIT License · © 2026 Michael A Wright · COR24-P24C · abc1234     |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+


Panel Descriptions
------------------

1. PASCAL SOURCE (left, top)
   - Read-only <pre> display with monospace font
   - Demo selector populates this panel with the demo's Pascal source
   - NOT editable — compiler runs at command line, not in browser
   - Shows the source that produced the .spc in the right panel
   - Catppuccin Mocha dark theme

2. P-CODE ASSEMBLY (right, top)
   - Read-only display of .spc output from the compiler
   - Populated when user clicks [Compile]
   - Shows the compiler's .spc output for the current Pascal source
   - If compilation fails, show error messages here instead
   - Scrollable, monospace

3. OUTPUT (left, bottom)
   - UART TX output from the running program
   - Cleared on each Run
   - UART input field + Send button for interactive programs (getc)

4. HARDWARE (right, bottom)
   - LED D2: visual indicator, on/off circle (sys 3 opcode)
   - Switch S2: toggle button (emulator button input)
   - Binary size: shows .p24 byte count after Link
   - Instruction count: COR24 instructions executed

5. STATUS BAR (top right)
   - Ready: compiled and linked, waiting for Run
   - Running: emulator executing (Run button becomes Stop)
   - Exited: program halted (via sys halt or trap)


Button Flow
-----------

[Demo...] — Select a demo program.
            1. Populate Pascal source panel (read-only) with .pas source
            2. Populate P-Code Assembly panel with pre-compiled .spc
            Both .pas and .spc are embedded via include_str!()
            Does NOT link or run automatically.

[Link & Run] — Link, assemble, load, and execute.
            1. Take pre-compiled .spc from the selected demo
            2. Combine with runtime .spc (pr24p, embedded via include_str!)
            3. Run pl24r to merge (Rust library call, fast in WASM)
            4. Run pa24r to assemble → .p24 (Rust library call, fast)
            5. Show binary size in Hardware panel
            6. Load pre-assembled pvm.s into emulator (from build time)
            7. Load .p24 binary into emulator memory at 0x010000
            8. Relocate data references (push instructions)
            9. Run pvm.s init, then patch code_base
            10. Start batch execution (50K instrs/tick, 25ms yield)
            11. Status → Running
            12. Button becomes [Stop]

[Stop]    — Pause execution
            1. Stop the tick timer
            2. Pause emulator
            3. Status → Exited (or Ready if not halted)

Note: Compile is NOT a button.  Compilation happens at the command line
using p24p.  The .spc files are pre-compiled and embedded in the binary.
Future: when p24p is available as a Rust/WASM library, add a [Compile]
button and make the Pascal source panel editable.


Demo Programs
-------------

Each demo consists of a .pas file (Pascal source, displayed read-only)
and a .spc file (pre-compiled p-code assembly, displayed read-only).
Both are embedded via include_str!().  The .spc is compiled from .pas
at the command line using p24p before building web-p24c.

1. Hello World
   program Hello;
   begin
     writeln(42)
   end.
   Expected output: 42\n

2. Countdown
   program Countdown;
   var i: integer;
   begin
     i := 5;
     while i > 0 do begin
       writeln(i);
       i := i - 1
     end
   end.
   Expected output: 5\n4\n3\n2\n1\n

3. LED Blink (when sys LED is available)
   program Blink;
   begin
     while true do begin
       led(1);
       { delay }
       led(0);
       { delay }
     end
   end.

4. Echo
   program Echo;
   var c: integer;
   begin
     c := getc;
     while c <> 113 do begin  { 'q' to quit }
       putc(c);
       c := getc
     end
   end.


Loading the VM (Same Pattern as web-dv24r)
-------------------------------------------

pvm.s (the p-code VM in COR24 assembly) is pre-assembled at build time
in build.rs using cor24-emulator's Assembler.  The binary and key label
addresses are embedded via include_bytes!/include!.

At runtime in WASM:
1. Init: load pvm.s binary into EmulatorCore, resolve label addresses
2. On Run: load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data refs, run pvm.s init
   (halt at code_seg), reset, set PC=vm_loop, fp=vm_state, patch
   code_base/pc/status in vm_state, resume

See web-dv24r's load_p24_image() and apply_pending_code_base() for the
exact implementation.  The loader must:
- Write code+data contiguously at load_addr (0x010000)
- Scan for push (0x01) instructions with IMM24 operands pointing into
  the data segment (>= code_size, < code_size + data_size)
- Relocate those operands by adding load_addr (absolute addressing)
- Write sys halt (0x60 0x00) at code_seg label
- Run pvm.s init (~261 COR24 instructions)
- reset() to clear halted flag (preserves memory)
- Set COR24 PC = vm_loop, fp = vm_state
- Patch vm_state: code=load_addr, pc=0, status=0
- Resume and run batches

Data relocation is needed because pvm.s's loadb/load/store opcodes
use ABSOLUTE memory addresses, not code-base-relative.


Batch Execution (Browser-Friendly)
------------------------------------

- 50,000 COR24 instructions per tick
- 25ms between ticks (yields to browser render thread)
- On each tick: feed UART bytes, run batch, collect UART output,
  check for halt/trap
- This prevents browser tab from becoming unresponsive


Catppuccin Mocha Theme
-----------------------

Same dark theme as web-dv24r and other COR24 web tools:
- Base: #1e1e2e
- Surface: #313244
- Text: #cdd6f4
- Green (success): #a6e3a1
- Red (error): #f38ba8
- Yellow (warning): #f9e2af
- Blue (info): #89b4fa
- Mauve (accent): #cba6f7

See web-dv24r's index.html and debugger.css for the complete theme.


Build Configuration
-------------------

Cargo.toml dependencies:
  cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
  pa24r = { path = "../../softwarewrighter/pa24r" }
  yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
  wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
  web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = [...] }
  gloo = "0.11"
  console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"

Build-dependencies (for pre-assembling pvm.s):
  cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }

Note: pl24r and pa24r are used at RUNTIME in WASM (linking and assembling
happen when user clicks Link).  cor24-emulator's Assembler is only used
at BUILD TIME (to pre-assemble pvm.s).

Rust edition 2024.  Trunk for WASM build.

Build commands:
  trunk build                    # Build WASM to dist/
  trunk serve --port 9199        # Dev server (use different port from web-dv24r 9198)
  cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
  cargo fmt --all


Pattern References
------------------

Study these projects for implementation patterns:

  ~/github/softwarewrighter/web-dv24r   P-code debugger (CLOSEST pattern)
    - build.rs: pre-assembles pvm.s, pre-assembles .spc demos
    - config.rs: PVM_BINARY, label_addr()
    - debugger.rs: load_p24_image(), apply_pending_code_base(),
      batch execution, UART I/O, EmulatorCore usage
    - demos.rs: include_bytes!() for pre-assembled .p24

  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tf24a    Forth debugger
    - Simplest Yew + EmulatorCore pattern
    - Demo loading via UART, batch execution

  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tc24r    C compiler UI
    - Closest UI pattern (editor + compiler output + run)
    - Has compile/run flow similar to what we need

  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tml24c   Lisp REPL
    - Tiered demo system, memory gauges


Related Projects
----------------

  ~/github/softwarewrighter/p24p      Pascal compiler (C, runs on COR24)
  ~/github/softwarewrighter/pr24p     Pascal runtime (.spc files)
  ~/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r     P-code linker (Rust library)
  ~/github/softwarewrighter/pa24r     P-code assembler (Rust library)
  ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/pv24a       P-code VM (COR24 assembly)
  ~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs          COR24 assembler + emulator


Wiki Coordination
-----------------

This project participates in multi-agent coordination via wiki at
http://localhost:7402.  See docs/agent-cas-wiki.md (copy from web-dv24r)
for the API reference.

Key wiki pages:
  [[MVP]]                  Current goal: Pascal Hello World at speed
  [[P24Toolchain]]         Pipeline overview
  [[PA24RSpec]]             P-code assembler spec
  [[COR24Architecture]]    Full architecture with mermaid diagrams
  [[AgentToAgentRequests]]  Cross-component dependency tracking
  [[AgentStatus]]           Who's online

On session start: read [[AgentToAgentRequests]] and [[AgentStatus]].
Update your status.  File requests for anything you need from other
agents.


MVP Scope
---------

For the first working version:
1. Read-only Pascal source display (demo programs only)
2. Read-only .spc display (pre-compiled, embedded)
3. Link via pl24r Rust library in WASM (fast)
4. Assemble via pa24r Rust library in WASM (fast)
5. Run via pvm.s on cor24-rs emulator (fast, pre-assembled at build time)
6. UART output display + input field
7. LED D2 indicator and S2 switch toggle
8. Execution status (Ready/Running/Exited)
9. 3-4 demo programs with .pas + .spc pairs
10. Binary size and instruction count display

Deferred:
- Editable Pascal source (needs p24p in browser)
- [Compile] button (needs p24p as Rust crate or WASM)
- Syntax highlighting
- Pascal compiler in Rust (replaces C-based p24p)
- File upload/download
- Source-level debugging (needs debug info in .p24)
- Multiple file support (user libraries)

1→.module runtime
     2→.export _p24p_write_int
     3→.export _p24p_write_bool
     4→.export _p24p_write_ln
     5→
     6→; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
     7→; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers
     8→
     9→; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
    10→; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
    11→; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
    12→; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
    13→; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
    14→; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
    15→.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    16→    loada 0              ; load argument n
    17→    ; check for negative
    18→    dup                  ; n n
    19→    push 0               ; n n 0
    20→    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    21→    jz positive
    22→    ; print minus sign
    23→    push 45              ; n '-'
    24→    sys 1                ; n
    25→    neg                  ; -n
    26→positive:
    27→    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    28→    push 0               ; push sentinel
    29→extract:
    30→    loadl 0              ; load n
    31→    push 10
    32→    mod                  ; n % 10
    33→    push 48
    34→    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    35→    loadl 0
    36→    push 10
    37→    div                  ; n / 10
    38→    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    39→    loadl 0
    40→    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
    41→wi_print:
    42→    dup
    43→    jz wi_done
    44→    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    45→    jmp wi_print
    46→wi_done:
    47→    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    48→    ret 1
    49→.end
    50→
    51→; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
    52→; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
    53→; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
    54→; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
    55→.proc _p24p_write_bool 0
    56→    loada 0              ; load argument b
    57→    jz wb_false
    58→    ; print "TRUE"
    59→    push 84              ; 'T'
    60→    sys 1
    61→    push 82              ; 'R'
    62→    sys 1
    63→    push 85              ; 'U'
    64→    sys 1
    65→    push 69              ; 'E'
    66→    sys 1
    67→    jmp wb_done
    68→wb_false:
    69→    ; print "FALSE"
    70→    push 70              ; 'F'
    71→    sys 1
    72→    push 65              ; 'A'
    73→    sys 1
    74→    push 76              ; 'L'
    75→    sys 1
    76→    push 83              ; 'S'
    77→    sys 1
    78→    push 69              ; 'E'
    79→    sys 1
    80→wb_done:

    82→.end
    83→
    84→; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )

    86→.proc _p24p_write_ln 0

file.content:
.module runtime
.export _p24p_write_int
.export _p24p_write_bool
.export _p24p_write_ln

; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers

; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    loada 0              ; load argument n
    ; check for negative
    dup                  ; n n
    push 0               ; n n 0
    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    jz positive
    ; print minus sign
    push 45              ; n '-'
    sys 1                ; n
    neg                  ; -n
positive:
    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    push 0               ; push sentinel
extract:
    loadl 0              ; load n
    push 10
    mod                  ; n % 10
    push 48
    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    loadl 0
    push 10
    div                  ; n / 10
    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    loadl 0
    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
wi_print:
    dup
    jz wi_done
    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    jmp wi_print
wi_done:
    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_bool 0
    loada 0              ; load argument b
    jz wb_false
    ; print "TRUE"
    push 84              ; 'T'
    sys 1
    push 82              ; 'R'
    sys 1
    push 85              ; 'U'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
    jmp wb_done
wb_false:
    ; print "FALSE"
    push 70              ; 'F'
    sys 1
    push 65              ; 'A'
    sys 1
    push 76              ; 'L'
    sys 1
    push 83              ; 'S'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
wb_done:
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )
; Print newline (LF) to UART via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_ln 0
    push 10              ; LF
    sys 1
    ret 0
.end

.endmodule

[package]
name = "web-p24c"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Michael A Wright"]
license = "MIT"
description = "Browser-based Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]

[dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
pa24r = { path = "../pa24r" }
pl24r = { path = "../pl24r" }
yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["console", "HtmlTextAreaElement", "HtmlInputElement", "HtmlSelectElement", "HtmlElement", "KeyboardEvent"] }
gloo = "0.11"
console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"

[build-dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = true

content.text: Good — I see the .spc format and the p-code instructions. Now let me also check the test for write_bool_ln to see an example with `.module` and `.extern`.

content.content:
1→.proc main 0

content.text:
Good, I have a clear picture now. The flow is:
1. `pl24r::parser::parse()` each .spc source
2. `pl24r::linker::link()` the modules
3. `pl24r::linker::emit()` the linked output
4. `pa24r::assemble_to_p24()` the linked .spc

Now let me create the demos and code. I need:
- `demos/hello.pas` + `demos/hello.spc` 
- `demos/countdown.pas` + `demos/countdown.spc`
- `src/demos.rs` - Demo struct with (name, pas_source, spc_source) + DEMOS + RUNTIME_SPC
- Update `src/lib.rs` to add demo selector dropdown

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/web-p24c/demos/countdown.pas

input.content:
.module countdown
.export main
.extern _p24p_write_int
.extern _p24p_write_ln

.global _g_i 1

.proc main 0
    ; i := 5
    push 5
    storeg _g_i
loop_test:
    ; while i > 0
    loadg _g_i
    push 0
    gt
    jz loop_end
    ; writeln(i)
    loadg _g_i
    call _p24p_write_int
    call _p24p_write_ln
    ; i := i - 1
    loadg _g_i
    push 1
    sub
    storeg _g_i
    jmp loop_test
loop_end:
    halt
.end

.endmodule
